repositoryPurpose: Data ingestion and synchronization service for Cross River Bank's lending and selling platform

primaryFeatures: Ingest loan servicing data from external MPLs (Marketplace Lenders) via S3 CSV files
Synchronize loan status between Arix and Selling databases
Import SOFR (Secured Overnight Financing Rate) data from Federal Reserve API
Generate metrics for CloudWatch dashboards to monitor lending operations
dataEntities: Loans - Represents consumer loans with loan_id, loan_number, status
Contracts - Represents lending agreements with MPLs including terms, fees, and rates
Servicing Values - Loan servicing data including adjusted_loan_amount and interest_paid
SOFR Rates - Federal Reserve interest rates (daily and 30-day average)
MPL Bootstrapping - Configuration for enabled marketplace lenders
Servicing Timestamps - Tracking when servicing data was last ingested by MPL

apis: Federal Reserve API - Used to fetch SOFR rates
AWS CloudWatch - Used for metrics reporting
dataStores: AWS S3 - Source for CSV servicing data files from MPLs
AWS Athena - Used to query data in data lake/warehouse
databases: Selling DB (PostgreSQL) - Main operational database where ingested data is stored
Loans DB (MSSQL) - External system containing Arix loan data
Contracts DB (MSSQL) - System of record for contract data
Volume DB (MSSQL) - System for tracking loan volumes
Vampire DB (MSSQL) - System with unclear purpose but requires data export/import

dataIngestion: S3 CSV file ingestion for servicing data from MPLs (Marlette, Momnt, etc.)
Federal Reserve API integration for SOFR rates
Database-to-database synchronization from MSSQL to PostgreSQL
dataTransformation: Conversion of CSV data to structured database records
Status reconciliation between Arix and Selling systems
Contract data bootstrapping from Contracts DB to Selling DB
metrics: Loan counts by MPL and loan type
utilities: AWS secret and SSM parameter management

modules: ingestion - Core data import and synchronization logic
models - SQLAlchemy ORM models for database tables
repository - Data access layer for each entity type
utils - Common utilities for AWS, DB access, logging

criticalFindings: The SOFR ingestion module fetches critical interest rate data that may be used for loan pricing
The sync module reconciles loan statuses between systems with specific attention to critical statuses (status >= 5)
The system imports contract data which defines financial terms between Cross River Bank and MPLs
The system handles loan purchase data which represents completed financial transactions
The repository includes special handling for bootstrapping MPL data which appears to be a critical initialization process
The repository references multiple external systems (Arix, Vampire) that may have complex dependencies

securityConsiderations.dataEncryption: The system handles sensitive financial data and uses AWS Secrets Manager for credential storage
securityConsiderations.databaseCredentials: Database credentials are managed through environment variables and AWS secrets

ambiguities: References to 'Vampire' system in multiple places but its specific purpose and API contract are unclear
The 'mpl_id' field is widely used but the list of valid MPLs is not explicitly defined in the code
Several loan status codes (e.g., 'status >= 5') are referenced without explanation of their business meaning
The relationship between 'Arix' and 'Selling' systems is not fully explained but appears to be critical
